rationale: The response wakes up to the repeated pattern of manual verification gates and explicitly names the prior fixes as door staffing, manual checking, and standing recheck. It does allow a fridge-side confirmer for today, so the first sentence is not a clean default refusal, but it frames this as a one-time emergency brake, prevents normalization, moves upstream to trigger conditions, process/system fields, responsibility chain, and escalation rules, and forbids continuing the N+1 add-a-person pattern. This satisfies the anti-spiral intent rather than directly adding another permanent manual gate.
